summary: Probably the best team at the tournament — an off-the-cuff 4-2-3-1 with front-four freedom and transition goals at pace. If it clicks, unstoppable.
style: 4-2-3-1 — front-four freedom to create, 1v1 wing play, fullbacks under/overlapping to cross, transition goals at pace, structure over press.

counterReliance.note: Transition goals at pace, structure over press

keyPlayers.name: Michael Olise
keyPlayers.note: Goals & creativity, a 1v1 specialist with work-rate & press

tactics: Free the front four, get wingers 1v1 with fullbacks crossing, and break at pace from a structured base. A vibe of combination play and improvisation — if they put it all together, hard to stop.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
